utility_evaluation.REASONING:
There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final plan includes all 5 top-level items. Now check if each is fully specified with all required details:
- health_coverage: Must be physical and mental health insurance with costs. Provided as two entries with named plans and explicit monthly costs (€100 and €65). This satisfies the requirement.
- property_coverage: Must be home/contents insurance with costs. Provided 'home_contents' with plan and cost (€60/month). Also includes 'adventure_equipment_protection' add-on with cost (€35/month). This satisfies the requirement.
- travel_coverage: Must be travel insurance with costs. Provided as a plan description with add-on and total monthly cost (€130/month). Satisfies the requirement.
- additional_services: Any enhancement services with costs. Provided 'insurance_portfolio_management' with a one-time cost (€300). Satisfies the requirement.
- total_budget: Complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. Provided monthly recurring cost (€390/month) and one-time cost (€300). It does not include an annual breakdown, but it does include a complete cost breakdown in monthly and one-time terms. The required format says 'complete monthly/annual cost breakdown'—annual is missing. This item is therefore not fully specified.
Thus, 4 items are fully specified out of 5.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final plan lists specific items: LifeFirst Standard Plan for physical and mental health; InsurePlus Standard Coverage for home contents; Retained Existing Add-on for adventure equipment (no matching ground-truth item, so it is excluded); LifeFirst Standard Plan + Adventure Travel Coverage add-on for travel (this corresponds to the LifeFirst Standard Plan in travel insurance and the Adventure Travel Coverage enhancement). Additional service is Insurance Portfolio Management (one-time). I mapped each named item to its exact counterpart in the ground-truth ratings and used the provided rating numbers.
